source: Limitless (Bankless) — AI supercycle; Unity as world-model builder; chips/memory/power
summary: Cerebras is a high-conviction inference chip bet, with early-stage entry validated by successful IPO
summary_extended: Baker identifies Cerebras as a high-conviction expression of his AI inference supercycle thesis, noting he was an early-stage investor in the company before its IPO. He highlights that Cerebras IPO'd at approximately $60 billion in valuation and has risen roughly 40% since its public debut, which he views as validation of both the company's positioning and his early entry thesis. Baker's core argument is that Cerebras solves a critical infrastructure bottleneck in AI inference — the delivery of fast, low-latency compute — making it a structural beneficiary as inference workloads scale dramatically. The investment implication is that Cerebras represents a differentiated chip architecture play on the inference supercycle, distinct from GPU-centric approaches, and Baker frames early-stage identification of such bottleneck solvers as a repeatable investment strategy.
detail: Baker was an early investor in Cerebras, which recently IPO'd at ~$60B and is up ~40% post-IPO. The investment expresses his inference supercycle thesis and his approach of identifying infrastructure bottleneck solvers early.
confidence_evidence: Early-stage investor, IPO validation, explicitly cited as core thesis expression
quote_fragment: Cerebras IPO'd for an ungodly amount, up 40% since

ticker_audit.reasoning: Cerebras Systems is the explicit subject of this thesis — Baker is an early investor and the entire thesis is about Cerebras's IPO performance and its role as an inference chip bottleneck solver. CBRS is the US-listed ticker for Cerebras post-IPO.
